title: Master Protocol: A 60-Minute Universal Green-Chemistry Workflow for RNA Extraction and Integrity Analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1lvfk","text":"This protocol is validated for Gram-Positive (GP) bacteria, Gram-Negative (GN) bacteria, and Yeast/Fungi.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1n86f","text":"General rules for ensuring consistent results across different microbial species.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":81}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2stqj","text":"Sterility is Paramount: Perform all steps in an RNase-free environment. Use DEPC-treated water for all reagent preparation and keep all tubes/tips autoclaved.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4offe","text":"Consistency in OD: For the best yield, ensure cultures are in the mid-log growth phase (OD₆₀₀ 0.8–1.0). Overgrown cultures (stationary phase) may have lower RNA quality due to increased RNase activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18},{"style":"bold","offset":88,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qsau","text":"Temperature Control: While most of the protocol is at room temperature, the Phase I Cooling Step (Step 6) must be performed strictly on ice to ensure proper precipitation of the RNA-protein-DNA complex.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20},{"style":"bold","offset":76,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66fof","text":"Mixing: Always mix by gentle pipetting or inverting during the RNA isolation phase. Avoid vigorous vortexing after Step 7 to prevent shearing of the RNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":22,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dlt75","text":"What the researcher needs to prepare before starting the 60-minute timer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":73}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8d294","text":"Reagent Prep: Prepare the Acidic Lysis Buffer (pH 5.0) and 5 mM Borax Buffer in advance. Borax dissolves easily in warm water but should be cooled to room temperature before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13},{"style":"bold","offset":26,"length":28},{"style":"bold","offset":59,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5h9nh","text":"Equipment Check: Set the water bath or heat block to 100°C at least 20 minutes before starting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16},{"style":"bold","offset":53,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rla8","text":"Ice: Ensure a fresh bucket of crushed ice is available for the 7-minute cooling step.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ou6p","text":"Gel Casting: To save time, cast the Borax-Agarose gel during the 10-minute resuspension or drying phases of Phase I.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":36,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"jihs","text":"Crucial hazards and PPE requirements.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":37}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"18qp7","text":"Thermal Hazard (Boiling Step): Step 4 requires incubation at 100°C. Use a secure heat block and handle tubes with insulated gloves or forceps to avoid steam burns. Ensure tubes are \"Safe-Lock\" style to prevent them from popping open during heating.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30},{"style":"bold","offset":61,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4j7hr","text":"Chemical Hazard (EtBr/Stains): If using Ethidium Bromide for visualization, handle only in a designated area. EtBr is a potent mutagen. Wear double nitrile gloves and dispose of all gels and tips in specialized hazardous waste containers.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30},{"style":"bold","offset":40,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d88vi","text":"Electrical Safety: Ensure the electrophoresis tank is properly closed and the power supply is dry before turning on the 120V current.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18},{"style":"bold","offset":120,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3mnrr","text":"UV Exposure: Use a UV shield or a closed imaging system when visualizing bands. Never look at the UV transilluminator directly with the naked eye.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Stain: GelRed, SYBR Safe, or Ethidium Bromide (as per lab safety colorless for high-resolution 5S rRNA detection).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61h7a","text":"2. Biological Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6scj2","text":"Bacterial/Yeast Cultures: Mid-log phase (OD₆₀₀ 0.8–1.0). Validated for S. aureus, Enterococcus spp., E. coli, and Yeast/Fungi.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25},{"style":"italic","offset":71,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":82,"length":17},{"style":"italic","offset":101,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":114,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7gc1k","text":"3. Equipment","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6uc1l","text":"Microcentrifuge: Capable of at least 16,000 xg.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5olgj","text":"Heat Block or Water Bath: Capable of maintaining 100°C (for boiling lysis).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25},{"style":"bold","offset":49,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5t45r","text":"Horizontal Electrophoresis System: Tank and power supply.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"27hqe","text":"UV Transilluminator or Blue Light Imaging System.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":49}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4jono","text":"Vortex Mixer.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ac741","text":"Microwave: For melting agarose.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6rjp8","text":"4. Consumables","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1qaq","text":"Microcentrifuge Tubes: 1.5 mL (Preferably \"Safe-Lock\" to prevent popping during the 100°C step).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2etr3","text":"Pipette Tips: RNase-free, filter tips recommended.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ec0i8","text":"Ice Bucket and Crushed Ice.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Harvest Cells: Centrifuge 500 µL of culture (OD₆₀₀ 0.8–1.0) at 7,000 rpm for 5 mins at room temperature. Discard supernatant.
Resuspension: Resuspend the pellet in 200 µL of autoclaved distilled water.
Lysis: Add 300 µL of acidic lysis buffer (pH 5.0). Mix gently by pipetting. Buffer Composition: 69.4 mM SDS, 68 mM sodium citrate dihydrate, 132 mM anhydrous citric acid, and 10 mM EDTA.
Cell Disruption (Boiling Step): Incubate the mixture at 100°C for 2 minutes. Note: This step is crucial for the robust cell walls of Gram-positive bacteria and yeast.
Initial Precipitation: Add 150 µL of precipitation buffer (4 M NaCl with citrate/citric acid). Invert 10 times.
Cooling: Incubate on ice for 7 minutes, then centrifuge at 16,000 xg for 3 mins.

Selective RNA Isolation: Transfer supernatant to a new tube containing 0.5 M Calcium Chloride (CaCl₂). Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes. Innovation: CaCl₂ precipitates RNA while keeping DNA and proteins in solution.
Pelleting: Centrifuge at 16,000 xg for 5 mins. Discard supernatant.
Wash 6 Dry: Wash the pellet with ice-cold 70% ethanol, centrifuge for 3 mins, and air dry for 10–20 mins.
Resuspension: Dissolve the dried RNA in 50 µL of autoclaved distilled water.
Prepare Gel: Dissolve 0.8%–1.0% agarose in 5 mM Borax solution (sodium tetraborate decahydrate).
Add Stain: Add GelRed or Ethidium Bromide to the melted agarose before casting.
Buffer: Use the same 5 mM Borax solution as the running buffer.
Borax acts as an "apparent denaturant," resolving RNA structures without pre-heating.
Sample Preparation: Mix 20 µL of RNA with 2 µL of 10X loading buffer. Pro-Tip: Use a colorless loading buffer (no Bromophenol Blue) for the sharpest visualization of small 5S rRNA bands.
Loading: Load samples directly onto the gel. No formaldehyde pre-treatment or cooling on ice is required.
Electrophoresis: Run at 120 V for 25 minutes. Comparison: This is significantly faster than standard TAE gels which require 60 minutes.
Visualization and Image Acquisition
Image Capture: After electrophoresis, place the gel on a UV transilluminator (302 nm or 312 nm) or a blue light imaging system.

Band Identification: For Bacteria (GP & GN): Look for two prominent, sharp bands. The upper band is the 23S rRNA (~2.9 kb) and the lower band is the 16S rRNA (~1.5 kb). For Yeast (Eukaryotic): Look for the 28S rRNA (upper band, ~3.8–4.8 kb) and the 18S rRNA (lower band, ~1.9–2.0 kb). Small RNA: A smaller, fainter band near the bottom of the gel represents the 5S rRNA and tRNAs.

Integrity Assessment: Ratio: In high-quality, intact RNA, the intensity of the upper band (23S/28S) should be approximately twice the intensity of the lower band (16S/18S). Degradation: A smear of low-molecular-weight fragments or the disappearance of the 23S/28S band indicates RNA degradation. DNA Contamination: High-molecular-weight bands near the wells indicate genomic DNA (gDNA) contamination.
creator: Abdulhadi Albaser
